# gRPC C++ Channel Creation API

This document covers the public C++ APIs for creating gRPC channels and instantiating stubs. It targets developers building gRPC clients.

---

## Overview

A **channel** is a connection to a gRPC server at a given address. Every gRPC stub wraps a channel. The typical workflow is:

1. Choose and construct `ChannelCredentials` (security policy).
2. Call `CreateChannel` (or `CreateCustomChannel` for advanced options) to get a `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`.
3. Pass the channel to a generated stub constructor, e.g. `MyService::NewStub(channel)`.

---

## Headers

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/create_channel.h>          // CreateChannel, CreateCustomChannel
#include <grpcpp/security/credentials.h>    // Credential factories
#include <grpcpp/support/channel_arguments.h> // ChannelArguments
```

---

## Channel Factory Functions

### `grpc::CreateChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds);
```

Creates a channel to `target` using the given credentials.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint, e.g. `"localhost:50051"` or `"dns:///example.com:443"`. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. If null or invalid, a *lame channel* is returned (all RPCs fail). |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::CreateCustomChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args);
```

Creates a channel with explicit `ChannelArguments` for fine-grained tuning.

> **Warning:** For advanced use and testing **only**. Override default channel arguments only when necessary.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. |
| `args` | `const ChannelArguments&` | Channel configuration options. |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>
grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args,
    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<
        grpc::experimental::ClientInterceptorFactoryInterface>>
        interceptor_creators);
```

Like `CreateCustomChannel`, but also installs per-call client interceptors.

> **Warning:** Experimental API — subject to change.

---

## Credential Types

### `grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials();
```

Returns credentials for an **unencrypted, unauthenticated** channel. Use only in development or on trusted private networks.

---

### `grpc::SslCredentials()`

```cpp
struct grpc::SslCredentialsOptions {
    grpc::string pem_root_certs;   // PEM-encoded CA root certs (empty = system defaults)
    grpc::string pem_private_key;  // PEM-encoded client private key (optional)
    grpc::string pem_cert_chain;   // PEM-encoded client certificate chain (optional)
};

std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::SslCredentials(
    const grpc::SslCredentialsOptions& options);
```

Returns TLS/SSL credentials. Supply `pem_private_key` and `pem_cert_chain` together for mutual TLS (mTLS). Leave them empty for server-only authentication.

---

### `grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials(
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& channel_creds,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::CallCredentials>& call_creds);
```

Combines channel-level credentials (e.g. SSL) with per-call credentials (e.g. an OAuth2 token). The composite applies both on every call.

---

### `grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials();
```

Application Default Credentials for Google services. Reads from `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS`, well-known config files, or the GCE metadata server.

> **Warning:** Only connect to Google endpoints with this credential type.

---

### `grpc::experimental::AltsCredentials()`

```cpp
struct grpc::experimental::AltsCredentialsOptions {
    std::vector<grpc::string> target_service_accounts; // Acceptable server identities
};

std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::experimental::AltsCredentials(
    const grpc::experimental::AltsCredentialsOptions& options);
```

[ALTS](https://cloud.google.com/security/encryption-in-transit/application-layer-transport-security) credentials for Google infrastructure mutual authentication.

---

### `grpc::experimental::LocalCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::experimental::LocalCredentials(
    grpc_local_connect_type type);
```

Credentials for local connections (`UDS` or loopback TCP). `type` is one of `LOCAL_TCP` or `UDS`.

---

### `grpc::XdsCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::XdsCredentials(
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& fallback_creds);
```

Credentials managed by the xDS control plane. Falls back to `fallback_creds` when xDS is not available.

---

## Call Credentials (per-call authentication)

These are attached to individual RPCs, either via `CompositeChannelCredentials` or directly on a `ClientContext`.

| Factory | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `grpc::AccessTokenCredentials(token)` | Static OAuth2 bearer token. |
| `grpc::GoogleComputeEngineCredentials()` | Automatically fetched GCE service account token. |
| `grpc::ServiceAccountJWTAccessCredentials(json_key, lifetime_s)` | Service account JWT, default lifetime 3600 s. |
| `grpc::GoogleRefreshTokenCredentials(json_refresh_token)` | Refreshes an OAuth2 token automatically. |
| `grpc::GoogleIAMCredentials(auth_token, authority_selector)` | Google IAM authorization token. |
| `grpc::MetadataCredentialsFromPlugin(plugin)` | User-defined metadata injection. |
| `grpc::ExternalAccountCredentials(json_string, scopes)` | Workload/workforce identity federation. |
| `grpc::experimental::StsCredentials(options)` | IETF Token Exchange (RFC 8693). |
| `grpc::CompositeCallCredentials(creds1, creds2)` | Chains two `CallCredentials` objects. |

---

## `grpc::ChannelArguments`

`ChannelArguments` carries low-level channel configuration passed to `CreateCustomChannel`.

```cpp
grpc::ChannelArguments args;
args.SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(16 * 1024 * 1024); // 16 MB
```

### Common Setters

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `SetSslTargetNameOverride(name)` | Override the SSL hostname used for certificate verification. Use with caution. |
| `SetCompressionAlgorithm(algorithm)` | Default compression algorithm for the channel (e.g. `GRPC_COMPRESS_GZIP`). |
| `SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(size)` | Maximum inbound message size in bytes (`-1` = unlimited). |
| `SetMaxSendMessageSize(size)` | Maximum outbound message size in bytes (`-1` = unlimited). |
| `SetUserAgentPrefix(prefix)` | String prepended to the `User-Agent` header. |
| `SetLoadBalancingPolicyName(name)` | LB policy name, e.g. `"round_robin"` or `"grpclb"`. |
| `SetServiceConfigJSON(json)` | Service config override as a JSON string (primarily for unit tests). |
| `SetResourceQuota(quota)` | Attach a `grpc::ResourceQuota` to cap memory usage. |
| `SetGrpclbFallbackTimeout(ms)` | Milliseconds to wait for a balancer before falling back to direct backends. |
| `SetSocketMutator(mutator)` | Low-level socket customization via a `grpc_socket_mutator`. |
| `SetInt(key, value)` | Generic integer argument (advanced). |
| `SetString(key, value)` | Generic string argument (advanced). |
| `SetPointer(key, value)` | Generic pointer argument — caller retains ownership (advanced). |
| `SetPointerWithVtable(key, value, vtable)` | Generic pointer argument — ownership transferred, vtable handles destruction (advanced). |

---

## Code Examples

### Example 1 — Insecure channel (local development)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "localhost:50051",
    grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials());

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 2 — TLS (server authentication only)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>

grpc::SslCredentialsOptions ssl_opts;
// pem_root_certs left empty → uses system default CA bundle
auto creds = grpc::SslCredentials(ssl_opts);

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel("example.com:443", creds);
auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 3 — Mutual TLS (mTLS)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include <fstream>
#include <sstream>

auto read_file = [](const std::string& path) {
    std::ifstream f(path);
    return std::string(std::istreambuf_iterator<char>(f), {});
};

grpc::SslCredentialsOptions ssl_opts;
ssl_opts.pem_root_certs  = read_file("ca.pem");
ssl_opts.pem_private_key = read_file("client.key");
ssl_opts.pem_cert_chain  = read_file("client.pem");

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "secure.example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(ssl_opts));

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 4 — Composite credentials (SSL + OAuth2 token)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>

auto channel_creds = grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions());
auto call_creds    = grpc::AccessTokenCredentials("ya29.my-oauth2-token");
auto composite     = grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials(channel_creds, call_creds);

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel("api.example.com:443", composite);
auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 5 — Custom channel with tuned arguments

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>

grpc::ChannelArguments args;
args.SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(64 * 1024 * 1024); // 64 MB
args.SetMaxSendMessageSize(64 * 1024 * 1024);
args.SetLoadBalancingPolicyName("round_robin");
args.SetUserAgentPrefix("my-client/1.0");

auto channel = grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    "backend.example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions()),
    args);

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 6 — Google Default Credentials (Cloud Run / GKE)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "pubsub.googleapis.com:443",
    grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials());

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

---

## Notes

- A **lame channel** is returned when `creds` is null or invalid. All RPCs on a lame channel fail immediately with `GRPC_STATUS_UNKNOWN`.
- `target` supports multiple URI schemes: bare `host:port`, `dns:///host:port`, `unix:path`, `ipv4:addr:port`, `ipv6:[addr]:port`, and xDS names.
- `ChannelArguments` is only needed with `CreateCustomChannel`; `CreateChannel` uses gRPC defaults.
- `grpc::experimental` APIs are subject to change without notice.

# gRPC C++ Channel Creation API

This document covers the public C++ APIs for creating gRPC channels and instantiating stubs. It targets developers building gRPC clients.

---

## Overview

A **channel** is a connection to a gRPC server at a given address. Every gRPC stub wraps a channel. The typical workflow is:

1. Choose and construct `ChannelCredentials` (security policy).
2. Call `CreateChannel` (or `CreateCustomChannel` for advanced options) to get a `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`.
3. Pass the channel to a generated stub constructor, e.g. `MyService::NewStub(channel)`.

---

## Headers

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/create_channel.h>              // CreateChannel, CreateCustomChannel
#include <grpcpp/security/credentials.h>        // Credential factories
#include <grpcpp/support/channel_arguments.h>   // ChannelArguments
```

---

## Channel Factory Functions

### `grpc::CreateChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds);
```

Creates a channel to `target` using the given credentials.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint, e.g. `"localhost:50051"` or `"dns:///example.com:443"`. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. If null or invalid, a *lame channel* is returned (all RPCs fail). |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::CreateCustomChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args);
```

Creates a channel with explicit `ChannelArguments` for fine-grained tuning.

> **Warning:** For advanced use and testing **only**. Override default channel arguments only when necessary.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. |
| `args` | `const ChannelArguments&` | Channel configuration options. |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>
grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args,
    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<
        grpc::experimental::ClientInterceptorFactoryInterface>>
        interceptor_creators);
```

Like `CreateCustomChannel`, but also installs per-call client interceptors.

> **Warning:** Experimental API — subject to change without notice.

---

## Credential Types

### Insecure — `grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials();
```

Returns credentials for an **unencrypted, unauthenticated** channel. Use only in development or on trusted private networks.

---

### SSL/TLS — `grpc::SslCredentials()`

```cpp
struct grpc::SslCredentialsOptions {
    grpc::string pem_root_certs;   // PEM-encoded CA root certs (empty = system defaults)
    grpc::string pem_private_key;  // PEM-encoded client private key (optional)
    grpc::string pem_cert_chain;   // PEM-encoded client certificate chain (optional)
};

std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::SslCredentials(
    const grpc::SslCredentialsOptions& options);
```

Returns TLS/SSL credentials. Supply `pem_private_key` and `pem_cert_chain` together for mutual TLS (mTLS). Leave them empty for server-only authentication with system CA roots.

---

### Composite — `grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials(
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& channel_creds,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::CallCredentials>& call_creds);
```

Combines channel-level credentials (e.g. SSL) with per-call credentials (e.g. an OAuth2 token). Both are applied on every RPC.

---

### Google Default — `grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials();
```

Application Default Credentials for Google services. Reads from the `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` environment variable, well-known config files, or the GCE metadata server.

> **Warning:** Only connect to Google endpoints with these credentials.

---

### ALTS — `grpc::experimental::AltsCredentials()`

```cpp
struct grpc::experimental::AltsCredentialsOptions {
    std::vector<grpc::string> target_service_accounts; // Acceptable server identities
};

std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::experimental::AltsCredentials(
    const grpc::experimental::AltsCredentialsOptions& options);
```

[Application Layer Transport Security](https://cloud.google.com/security/encryption-in-transit/application-layer-transport-security) credentials for mutual authentication on Google infrastructure.

---

### Local — `grpc::experimental::LocalCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::experimental::LocalCredentials(
    grpc_local_connect_type type);  // LOCAL_TCP or UDS
```

Credentials for local connections over Unix Domain Sockets or loopback TCP.

---

### XDS — `grpc::XdsCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::XdsCredentials(
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& fallback_creds);
```

Credentials managed by the xDS control plane. Falls back to `fallback_creds` when xDS is unavailable.

---

## Call Credentials (per-call authentication)

`CallCredentials` attach authentication metadata to individual RPCs. They can be combined with channel credentials via `CompositeChannelCredentials`, or applied directly on a `ClientContext`.

| Factory | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `grpc::AccessTokenCredentials(token)` | Static OAuth2 bearer token. |
| `grpc::GoogleComputeEngineCredentials()` | Automatically fetched GCE service account token. |
| `grpc::ServiceAccountJWTAccessCredentials(json_key, lifetime_s)` | Service account JWT; default lifetime 3600 s (max). |
| `grpc::GoogleRefreshTokenCredentials(json_refresh_token)` | Automatically refreshed OAuth2 token. |
| `grpc::GoogleIAMCredentials(auth_token, authority_selector)` | Google IAM authorization token. |
| `grpc::MetadataCredentialsFromPlugin(plugin)` | User-defined metadata injection via `MetadataCredentialsPlugin`. |
| `grpc::ExternalAccountCredentials(json_string, scopes)` | Workload/workforce identity federation. |
| `grpc::experimental::StsCredentials(options)` | IETF Token Exchange (RFC 8693). |
| `grpc::CompositeCallCredentials(creds1, creds2)` | Chains two `CallCredentials` objects together. |

---

## `grpc::ChannelArguments`

`ChannelArguments` carries low-level channel configuration passed to `CreateCustomChannel`. Construct one, call setters as needed, then pass it by const-reference.

```cpp
grpc::ChannelArguments args;
args.SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(16 * 1024 * 1024);
```

### Common Setters

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `SetSslTargetNameOverride(name)` | Override the SSL hostname used for certificate verification. Use with caution in production. |
| `SetCompressionAlgorithm(algorithm)` | Default compression for the channel (e.g. `GRPC_COMPRESS_GZIP`). |
| `SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(size)` | Maximum inbound message size in bytes (`-1` = unlimited). |
| `SetMaxSendMessageSize(size)` | Maximum outbound message size in bytes (`-1` = unlimited). |
| `SetUserAgentPrefix(prefix)` | String prepended to the `User-Agent` header sent to the server. |
| `SetLoadBalancingPolicyName(name)` | LB policy, e.g. `"round_robin"` or `"grpclb"`. |
| `SetServiceConfigJSON(json)` | Service config override as a JSON string (primarily for unit tests). |
| `SetResourceQuota(quota)` | Attach a `grpc::ResourceQuota` to cap channel memory usage. |
| `SetGrpclbFallbackTimeout(ms)` | Milliseconds to wait for a balancer response before falling back to direct backends. |
| `SetSocketMutator(mutator)` | Low-level socket customization via a `grpc_socket_mutator`. |
| `SetInt(key, value)` | Generic integer argument for advanced/internal options. |
| `SetString(key, value)` | Generic string argument for advanced/internal options. |
| `SetPointer(key, value)` | Generic pointer argument — caller retains ownership. |
| `SetPointerWithVtable(key, value, vtable)` | Generic pointer argument — ownership transferred; vtable handles destruction. |

---

## Code Examples

### Example 1 — Insecure channel (local development)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "localhost:50051",
    grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials());

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 2 — TLS with system CA roots (server authentication only)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

// Empty SslCredentialsOptions uses the system default CA bundle.
auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions()));

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 3 — Mutual TLS (mTLS)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include <fstream>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

auto read_file = [](const std::string& path) -> std::string {
    std::ifstream f(path);
    return {std::istreambuf_iterator<char>(f), {}};
};

grpc::SslCredentialsOptions ssl_opts;
ssl_opts.pem_root_certs  = read_file("ca.pem");
ssl_opts.pem_private_key = read_file("client.key");
ssl_opts.pem_cert_chain  = read_file("client.pem");

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "secure.example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(ssl_opts));

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 4 — Composite credentials (SSL + OAuth2 access token)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

auto channel_creds = grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions());
auto call_creds    = grpc::AccessTokenCredentials("ya29.my-oauth2-token");
auto composite     = grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials(channel_creds, call_creds);

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel("api.example.com:443", composite);
auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 5 — Custom channel with tuned arguments

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

grpc::ChannelArguments args;
args.SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(64 * 1024 * 1024); // 64 MB
args.SetMaxSendMessageSize(64 * 1024 * 1024);
args.SetLoadBalancingPolicyName("round_robin");
args.SetUserAgentPrefix("my-client/1.0");

auto channel = grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    "backend.example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions()),
    args);

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 6 — Google Default Credentials (Cloud Run / GKE)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

// Reads credentials automatically from the environment.
auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "pubsub.googleapis.com:443",
    grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials());

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

---

## Notes

- A **lame channel** is returned when `creds` is null or invalid. All RPCs on a lame channel fail immediately.
- `target` supports multiple URI schemes: bare `host:port`, `dns:///host:port`, `unix:path`, `ipv4:addr:port`, `ipv6:[addr]:port`, and xDS names.
- `ChannelArguments` is only used with `CreateCustomChannel`; `CreateChannel` uses gRPC's built-in defaults.
- APIs under `grpc::experimental` are subject to change without notice.

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/grpc--v1.68.0` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grpc--v1.68.0$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# gRPC C++ Channel Creation API

This document covers the public C++ APIs for creating gRPC channels and instantiating stubs. It targets developers building gRPC clients.

---

## Overview

A **channel** is a connection to a gRPC server at a given address. Every gRPC stub wraps a channel. The typical workflow is:

1. Choose and construct `ChannelCredentials` (security policy).
2. Call `CreateChannel` (or `CreateCustomChannel` for advanced options) to get a `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`.
3. Pass the channel to a generated stub constructor, e.g. `MyService::NewStub(channel)`.

---

## Headers

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/create_channel.h>          // CreateChannel, CreateCustomChannel
#include <grpcpp/security/credentials.h>    // Credential factories
#include <grpcpp/support/channel_arguments.h> // ChannelArguments
```

---

## Channel Factory Functions

### `grpc::CreateChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds);
```

Creates a channel to `target` using the given credentials.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint, e.g. `"localhost:50051"` or `"dns:///example.com:443"`. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. If null or invalid, a *lame channel* is returned (all RPCs fail). |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::CreateCustomChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args);
```

Creates a channel with explicit `ChannelArguments` for fine-grained tuning.

> **Warning:** For advanced use and testing **only**. Override default channel arguments only when necessary.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. |
| `args` | `const ChannelArguments&` | Channel configuration options. |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>
grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args,
    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<
        grpc::experimental::ClientInterceptorFactoryInterface>>
        interceptor_creators);
```

Like `CreateCustomChannel`, but also installs per-call client interceptors.

> **Warning:** Experimental API — subject to change.

---

## Credential Types

### `grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials()`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::InsecureChannelCredentials();
```

Returns credentials for an **unencrypted, unauthenticated** channel. Use only in development or on trusted private networks.

---

### `grpc::SslCredentials()`

```cpp
struct grpc::SslCredentialsOptions {
    grpc::string pem_root_certs;   // PEM-encoded CA root certs (empty = system defaults)
    grpc::string pem_private_key;  // PEM-encoded client private key (optional)
    grpc::string pem_cert_chain;   // PEM-encoded client certificate chain (optional)
};

std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials> grpc::SslCredentials(
```

## Overview

A **channel** is a connection to a gRPC server at a given address. Every gRPC stub wraps a channel. The typical workflow is:

1. Choose and construct `ChannelCredentials` (security policy).
2. Call `CreateChannel` (or `CreateCustomChannel` for advanced options) to get a `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`.
3. Pass the channel to a generated stub constructor, e.g. `MyService::NewStub(channel)`.

---

## Headers

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/create_channel.h>              // CreateChannel, CreateCustomChannel
#include <grpcpp/security/credentials.h>        // Credential factories
#include <grpcpp/support/channel_arguments.h>   // ChannelArguments
```

---

### `grpc::CreateChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds);
```

Creates a channel to `target` using the given credentials.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint, e.g. `"localhost:50051"` or `"dns:///example.com:443"`. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. If null or invalid, a *lame channel* is returned (all RPCs fail). |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::CreateCustomChannel`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel> grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args);
```

Creates a channel with explicit `ChannelArguments` for fine-grained tuning.

> **Warning:** For advanced use and testing **only**. Override default channel arguments only when necessary.

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `target` | `const grpc::string&` | URI of the server endpoint. |
| `creds` | `std::shared_ptr<ChannelCredentials>` | Security credentials. |
| `args` | `const ChannelArguments&` | Channel configuration options. |

**Returns:** `std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>`

---

### `grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors`

```cpp
std::shared_ptr<grpc::Channel>
grpc::experimental::CreateCustomChannelWithInterceptors(
    const grpc::string& target,
    const std::shared_ptr<grpc::ChannelCredentials>& creds,
    const grpc::ChannelArguments& args,
    std::vector<std::unique_ptr<

        interceptor_creators);
```

```cpp
    grpc::string pem_cert_chain;   // PEM-encoded client certificate chain (optional)
```

```cpp

#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"
```

```cpp
// Empty SslCredentialsOptions uses the system default CA bundle.
auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions()));

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 3 — Mutual TLS (mTLS)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include <fstream>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

auto read_file = [](const std::string& path) -> std::string {
    std::ifstream f(path);
    return {std::istreambuf_iterator<char>(f), {}};
};

grpc::SslCredentialsOptions ssl_opts;
ssl_opts.pem_root_certs  = read_file("ca.pem");
ssl_opts.pem_private_key = read_file("client.key");
ssl_opts.pem_cert_chain  = read_file("client.pem");

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "secure.example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(ssl_opts));

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 4 — Composite credentials (SSL + OAuth2 access token)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

auto channel_creds = grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions());
auto call_creds    = grpc::AccessTokenCredentials("ya29.my-oauth2-token");
auto composite     = grpc::CompositeChannelCredentials(channel_creds, call_creds);

auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel("api.example.com:443", composite);
auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 5 — Custom channel with tuned arguments

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

grpc::ChannelArguments args;
args.SetMaxReceiveMessageSize(64 * 1024 * 1024); // 64 MB
args.SetMaxSendMessageSize(64 * 1024 * 1024);
args.SetLoadBalancingPolicyName("round_robin");
args.SetUserAgentPrefix("my-client/1.0");

auto channel = grpc::CreateCustomChannel(
    "backend.example.com:443",
    grpc::SslCredentials(grpc::SslCredentialsOptions()),
    args);

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

### Example 6 — Google Default Credentials (Cloud Run / GKE)

```cpp
#include <grpcpp/grpcpp.h>
#include "myservice.grpc.pb.h"

// Reads credentials automatically from the environment.
auto channel = grpc::CreateChannel(
    "pubsub.googleapis.com:443",
    grpc::GoogleDefaultCredentials());

auto stub = MyService::NewStub(channel);
```

---

## Notes

- A **lame channel** is returned when `creds` is null or invalid. All RPCs on a lame channel fail immediately.
- `target` supports multiple URI schemes: bare `host:port`, `dns:///host:port`, `unix:path`, `ipv4:addr:port`, `ipv6:[addr]:port`, and xDS names.
- `ChannelArguments` is only used with `CreateCustomChannel`; `CreateChannel` uses gRPC's built-in defaults.
- APIs under `grpc::experimental` are subject to change without notice.
